scope: Pass B full-surface expansion. The ServiceNow Table API is a single uniform REST surface (GET list / GET by sys_id / POST insert / PUT replace / PATCH partial-update / DELETE) applied identically to every table name; this connector scopes that uniform surface to the 3 legacy-parity tables (incident, sys_user, sys_user_group) rather than every possible ServiceNow table (there is no finite enumeration of customer-defined tables to declare). Every documented Table API operation is accounted for below per table, covered_by a stream/write or excluded with a real reason. capabilities.write flips to true for the 3 new create/update write actions (create_<table>/update_<table>); delete is excluded per-table as destructive_admin (ServiceNow Table API DELETE is a hard, unrecoverable delete gated by delete ACL, not a soft/reversible operation, and legacy never exposed any mutation capability at all) rather than implemented — see docs.md Write actions & risks for the full reasoning on why create/update were added but delete was not.

excluded.reason: single-record detail fetch for a resource the incidents stream (GET /api/now/table/incident) already lists in full; no additional field is reachable only through this endpoint
single-record detail fetch for a resource the users stream (GET /api/now/table/sys_user) already lists in full
single-record detail fetch for a resource the groups stream (GET /api/now/table/sys_user_group) already lists in full

ServiceNow's Table API documents PUT and PATCH as functionally identical on this resource (both modify only the fields present in the request body, unlike standard REST PUT's whole-resource replace semantics) — update_incident (PATCH) already covers this exact capability; declaring both would send byte-identical requests for any accepted input
same PUT/PATCH functional-identity reasoning as incident; update_user (PATCH) already covers it
same PUT/PATCH functional-identity reasoning as incident; update_group (PATCH) already covers it

a hard, unrecoverable delete of an incident record gated by ServiceNow's own delete ACL; legacy exposed zero mutation capability (Capabilities{Write:false}) and this expansion's write additions are scoped to the reversible/low-risk create+update surface, not destructive deletes

a hard, unrecoverable delete of a user account record gated by ServiceNow's own delete ACL; deleting a sys_user row can cascade-orphan every record that references it (assigned incidents, group membership, approvals) and is normally performed only through ServiceNow's own admin UI, not an unattended API mutation
a hard, unrecoverable delete of a group record gated by ServiceNow's own delete ACL; deleting a group can orphan every membership/assignment rule that references it

group-membership join table not implemented by legacy and not one of this migration's 3 legacy-parity tables; a future capability expansion could add it as a 4th table following the identical stream+write shape used here
the separate Aggregate API (COUNT/SUM/AVG/MIN/MAX over a table) is a distinct API surface from the Table API this connector targets; not implemented by legacy
the separate Import Set API for staged bulk loads is a distinct API surface from the Table API this connector targets; not implemented by legacy

the separate Attachment API returns/accepts binary file payloads (multipart upload, raw byte download), not JSON table rows; the engine's declarative HTTP dialect has no multipart/binary-body primitive
